title: Owner can drain all APE tokens before claim period starts via claimUnclaimedTokens
claim: The claimUnclaimedTokens() function uses the check `block.timestamp > claimStartTime + claimDuration` to determine if the claim period has ended. Before startClaimablePeriod() is called, claimStartTime and claimDuration both default to 0, so the sum is 0. Since block.timestamp is always > 0, this check always passes, allowing the owner to withdraw all APE tokens from the contract immediately after deployment, before any user can claim.
impact: The owner can perform a complete rug-pull: deploy the contract, fund it with APE tokens, and immediately call claimUnclaimedTokens() to drain all funds before the airdrop even begins. Users would have zero tokens to claim. Total potential loss: up to ~150M APE (the full airdrop allocation).

paths.description: Owner drains all APE before claim period starts
steps: 1. Deploy AirdropGrapesToken contract (constructor calls _pause(), sets claimStartTime=0, claimDuration=0)
2. Transfer APE tokens to the contract address
3. Call claimUnclaimedTokens() - the check `block.timestamp > 0 + 0` always passes
4. All APE tokens are transferred to owner()
5. Users can never claim anything - the contract is drained

title: NFTX vault flash-loan attack via onERC721Received callback enables claiming airdrop for temporarily held NFTs
claim: The claimTokens() function grants airdrop tokens based on instantaneous NFT ownership at call time, without any holding period, snapshot, or reentrancy guard. An attacker can exploit this by redeeming unclaimed NFTs from an NFTX vault, receiving them via ERC721 safeTransfer which triggers onERC721Received, calling claimTokens() inside that callback to claim the airdrop, and then returning the NFTs to the vault - all atomically in one transaction. The attacker earns the airdrop reward (10,094 APE per BAYC + 856 APE per paired BAKC = 10,950 APE per pair) without needing to actually own the NFTs.
impact: Attackers can 'mine' the airdrop by flash-borrowing unclaimed NFTs from NFTX vaults. Each BAYC+BAKC pair yields 10,950 APE in profit (minus gas and vault fees). This can be repeated for every unclaimed NFT in the vault, diverting airdrop rewards from legitimate long-term holders to opportunistic attackers.

paths.description: NFTX vault flash-loan attack for maximum profit
steps: 1. Attacker identifies unclaimed BAYC token IDs held in NFTX BAYC vault
2. Attacker acquires NFTX vault tokens (or flash-loans them)
3. Attacker calls vault.redeem(amount, [unclaimedTokenIds]) to withdraw specific unclaimed BAYCs
4. The vault transfers BAYCs to attacker, triggering onERC721Received on attacker's contract
5. In onERC721Received callback, attacker calls AirdropGrapesToken.claimTokens()
6. claimTokens() sees the newly-received BAYCs + any BAKCs attacker holds, computes rewards, and transfers APE to attacker
7. Attacker returns BAYCs to vault via vault.mint([tokenIds], [amounts])
8. Attacker repays flash-loan if used, keeps APE profit
Optimal: Redeem N unclaimed BAYC when holding N unclaimed BAKC → get N*(10,094+856) = N*10,950 APE

optimal_params.strategy: Redeem N unclaimed BAYC from NFTX vault while holding N unclaimed BAKC to maximize gamma claims

title: claimUnclaimedTokens lacks pause modifier allowing owner to drain tokens while claims are paused
claim: The claimUnclaimedTokens() function has no whenNotPaused or whenPaused modifier, unlike claimTokens() which requires whenNotPaused and startClaimablePeriod() which requires whenPaused. This means the owner can call pauseClaimablePeriod() to halt all user claims, then call claimUnclaimedTokens() (if the claim period has ended) to withdraw remaining tokens while users are blocked from claiming. Additionally, it creates an inconsistent state where the owner can withdraw tokens during an active but paused claim period if the deadline has passed.
impact: The owner can selectively block users from claiming by pausing the contract, wait for the claim deadline to pass, and then withdraw all remaining tokens. This undermines the trust model of the airdrop and allows the owner to capture tokens that rightfully belong to late-claiming users.

paths.description: Owner pauses claims and drains remaining tokens
steps: 1. Owner calls pauseClaimablePeriod() during active claim period
2. Users can no longer call claimTokens() (reverts with 'Pausable: paused')
3. Owner waits until block.timestamp > claimStartTime + claimDuration
4. Owner calls claimUnclaimedTokens() - succeeds because no pause modifier
5. All remaining APE tokens sent to owner, users who were blocked cannot claim

title: Users who claim alpha/beta NFTs without holding gamma permanently lose gamma claiming capability
claim: The gamma (BAKC) claiming mechanism caps claims at `min(unclaimedAlpha + unclaimedBeta, unclaimedGamma)`. The claimTokens() function marks ALL unclaimed alpha/beta as claimed first, then claims gamma up to the cap. If a user holds alpha/beta but no gamma at claim time, the alpha/beta are marked claimed without any gamma being claimed. Those alpha/beta 'slots' are permanently consumed, and the user can never use them to claim gamma later, even if they subsequently acquire gamma tokens.
impact: Users who claim their BAYC/MAYC airdrop before acquiring BAKC tokens permanently lose the ability to claim BAKC rewards that those BAYC/MAYC would have entitled them to. Each BAKC reward is worth 856 APE, so a user with 1 BAYC who claims early before getting 1 BAKC loses 856 APE permanently.

paths.description: User permanently loses gamma rewards by claiming alpha/beta first
steps: 1. User holds 1 BAYC (unclaimed) but 0 BAKC
2. User calls claimTokens() → gets 10,094 APE for BAYC, BAYC marked claimed
3. User later acquires 1 BAKC (unclaimed)
4. User calls claimTokens() again → unclaimedAlpha=0, unclaimedGamma=1
5. gammaToBeClaim = min(0, 1) = 0 → gets 0 APE for BAKC
6. The BAKC can never be claimed by this user (unless they acquire more unclaimed BAYC/MAYC)
Loss: 856 APE per orphaned BAKC
